spec_paraphrase: AA-SVD: Anchored Adaptive SVD (2604.02119), Theorem 3.2, Corollary 3.3
paper_verbatim: AA-SVD: Anchored and Adaptive SVD for Large Language Model Compression
notes: Spec drops the 'and' between 'Anchored' and 'Adaptive' but the acronym and intent are correct.

spec_paraphrase: Cross-covariance C = X_pre^T X_post captures 'what the original model would have produced for the inputs the compressed model actually receives' — same dual-anchoring principle.
paper_verbatim: anchoring each compressed layer to the original outputs while explicitly modeling input distribution shifts
notes: Spec adopts dual-anchoring at the cross-covariance level.

spec_paraphrase: Spec Path 1 instantiates objective (4) anchored-adaptive ‖WX − W'X'‖²_F via Theorem 3.2 with cross-covariance C.
paper_verbatim: 4 Anchored adaptive (ours): ‖WX − W'X'‖²_F
notes: Spec's primary path is the anchored-adaptive objective (4). Note: paper's §4.3 recommends objective (2) input-aware + block refinement instead; see #paper_recommendation.

spec_paraphrase: M = W·C·B⁻¹·L_B then SVD_k then back-solve via L_B⁻¹ = exact Fig.2 closed form with C=XX'^T, B=X'X'^T, L_B from eigh.
notes: Spec uses eigendecomposition-based square root for L_B (paper allows Cholesky or EVD).

spec_paraphrase: (none — spec has no block-level refinement step)
notes: Stage 3 implements only the layer-wise factorization; no block-level loss is optimized. Paper §6 calls block-refinement 'more critical' than the layer objective itself.

spec_paraphrase: Spec's cross-covariance gloss is the dual-anchoring restated.
paper_verbatim: outputs should remain close to those of the uncompressed model, and it must operate correctly on the inputs it will actually receive at inference time

spec_paraphrase: (spec compresses gate→up→down per-expert independently; no block-output joint refinement).
paper_verbatim: errors across the multiple linear layers within a transformer block can interact... We therefore introduce a block-level refinement step
notes: Block-level refinement is wholly absent from §6 Stage 3. Paper says it is required to prevent compounding.

spec_paraphrase: W ≈ U·V where U ∈ ℝ^{d_out × k}, V ∈ ℝ^{k × d_in}.
paper_verbatim: W' = UV^T with U ∈ R^{m×k} and V ∈ R^{n×k}
notes: Storage layout matches (modulo transpose convention).

spec_paraphrase: SVD truncation U_k = U[:,:k]·diag(Σ[:k]) is the EYM optimal rank-k Frobenius approximation.
paper_verbatim: min_{rank(W')≤k} ‖W − W'‖²_F = Σ_{i>k} σ_i(W)²

spec_paraphrase: Spec Path 1 solves min ‖WC − W'B‖² implicitly via the closed form.
paper_verbatim: min_{rank(W')≤k} ‖W A − W' B‖²_F

spec_paraphrase: M = W·C·B⁻¹·L_B; SVD_k(M); back-solve by L_B⁻¹.
paper_verbatim: W'⋆ = SVD_k( W A B^T (B B^T)^{-1} L_B ) L_B^{-1}
notes: Identification: A↔X_pre, B↔X_post, AB^T↔C, BB^T↔B.

spec_paraphrase: (spec cites the theorem but doesn't reproduce the proof — acceptable)
paper_verbatim: ‖W' L_B − W A B^T L_B^{-T}‖²_F − ...
notes: Proof not required in spec.

spec_paraphrase: Eigendecomposition replaces Cholesky (handles rank-deficient B natively); dtype-aware noise floor (bf16→1e-2, fp16→1e-3, fp32→1e-6); k_eff = min(k, r_eff).
paper_verbatim: replace L_B^{-1} by the Moore–Penrose factor (BB^T)^{+1/2}, or equivalently use a Tikhonov-regularized factorization BB^T + εI = L_ε L_ε^T
notes: Spec uses dtype-keyed eigenvalue noise floor and effective-rank truncation rather than Tikhonov εI; numerically equivalent in spirit (zero-out small/negative eigenvalues). Both achieve the rank-deficient safeguard the paper recommends.

spec_paraphrase: Path 3 — Corollary 3.3 fallback: M = W · L_B (B-only).
paper_verbatim: W'⋆ = SVD_k(W L_B) L_B^{-1}
notes: Used as down_proj fallback under D6.

spec_paraphrase: (spec doesn't discuss SVD-LLM/SVD-LLM V2 lineage)
paper_verbatim: SVD-LLM uses the lower-triangular Cholesky factor L_X, while SVD-LLM V2 uses the eigendecomposition
notes: Spec uses eigh (matches SVD-LLM V2 style) but doesn't cite this lineage.

spec_paraphrase: Spec Path 1 implements ‖WX − W'X'‖²_F.
paper_verbatim: min_{W' : rank(W')=k} ‖W X − W' X'‖²_F

spec_paraphrase: Spec accumulates B = X_post^T X_post and C = X_pre^T X_post per batch and spills per-layer (streaming).
paper_verbatim: operates only on the covariance matrices XX'^T and X'X'^T, not on raw activations, so its cost is independent of the number of calibration tokens

spec_paraphrase: Per-(layer, expert, matrix) factor takes W, C, B, k.
paper_verbatim: Require: Weight matrix W, original inputs X, shifted inputs X', target rank k

spec_paraphrase: Path 1 = AA-SVD (A=X_pre, B=X_post); Path 3 = B-only (Cor 3.3, A=B=X_post).
paper_verbatim: Set A = X, B = X' {shift-aware: A=B=X'; input-aware: A=B=X}

spec_paraphrase: Compute C = X_pre^T X_post (cross) and B = X_post^T X_post (auto).
paper_verbatim: Compute C = A B^T and S = B B^T

spec_paraphrase: L_B = eigendecomposition-based square root of B; eigh allowed by paper.
paper_verbatim: Factorize: S = R R^T {e.g. Cholesky or EVD}

spec_paraphrase: M = W · C · B⁻¹ · L_B.
paper_verbatim: Compute M = W C S^{-1} R

spec_paraphrase: SVD(M) → U Σ V^T; truncate to rank k.
paper_verbatim: Truncated SVD: [U_k, Σ_k, V_k] = SVD_k(M)

spec_paraphrase: U_k = U[:,:k]·diag(Σ[:k]); V_k^T = V^T[:k,:] · L_B⁻¹; W ≈ U_k·V_k^T.
paper_verbatim: U = U_k Σ_k, V = R^{-T} V_k, so that W' = U V^T
notes: Equivalent factorization (V_k^T·L_B⁻¹ = (R^{-T} V_k)^T).

spec_paraphrase: (spec does not iterate Algorithm 2; instead collects covariance via single dual-forward pass with both teacher and student fixed.)
paper_verbatim: Extract input activations X ← X' ← E(calibration data) from the embedding layer E
notes: Spec uses post-Stage-2.5 student as a fixed 'partially compressed' state; does not propagate X' block-by-block as Alg.2 does. Acceptable simplification because Stage 2/2.5 already produced the shifted-input distribution before Stage 3 begins. Not strictly the paper's iterative scheme.

spec_paraphrase: (spec compresses gate/up/down per-expert independently from a single covariance pass; no per-layer X'_j re-collection inside the loop.)
paper_verbatim: Collect X_j from L_i and X'_j from L'_i by forward pass up to layer j ... Update W_j ← U_j V_j^T
notes: Paper requires re-collecting X'_j AFTER each prior layer is compressed (X' depends on already-compressed predecessors within the same block). Spec collects all covariances once before any factorization, so within-block sequential dependency is ignored. Stage 3's gate/up share covariance optimization (per #shared_covariance) is consistent only with this single-pass scheme.

spec_paraphrase: (no Stage-3 block-level refinement step; norms are listed as protected components, line 672)
paper_verbatim: Block-level refinement: optimize {U_j, V_j} and block-local parameters θ_i (e.g. norms, biases) jointly to minimize MSE(L_i(X), L'_i(X'))
notes: Paper's most critical contribution is omitted from Stage 3. §10 'Protected Components' explicitly forbids modifying layer norms, contradicting the paper's prescription that θ_i (norm scales/biases) are trainable during refinement. No Ch.12 row sanctions this omission.

spec_paraphrase: (spec doesn't iterate per-block; Stage 2.5 KD already produced the 'compressed-so-far' state and Stage 3 freezes it before factoring.)
paper_verbatim: Update inputs for next block: X ← L_i(X), X' ← L'_i(X')
notes: Lock-step dual-stream propagation absent; subsumed by Stage 2.5 and the dual-forward in Phase A. Practical effect: covariance for layer i+1 is collected against the *un*-factored layer i student, not the factored layer i student — slight mismatch with paper's prescription.

spec_paraphrase: (no block objective)
paper_verbatim: min_{{U_j, V_j}, θ_i} E_{X∼D_i} ‖L_i(X) − L'_i(X')‖²_F
notes: Same as #alg2_refine — block objective absent from spec.

spec_paraphrase: Layer norms (RMSNorm) are listed under 'Protected Components' — never modified by any compression stage.
paper_verbatim: θ_i denotes the remaining trainable parameters of the block (e.g., normalization scales and biases). The optimization is thus over all factorized weights and block-local parameters jointly.
notes: Spec actively forbids what the paper requires for Stage 3 block refinement. Direct contradiction with the paper-faithful recipe.

spec_paraphrase: (Stage 3 §6 doesn't explicitly state calibration sample count for AA-SVD covariance; reuses Stage 2 calibration set.)
paper_verbatim: we follow prior work and use 256 samples drawn from the WikiText2 (Merity et al., 2016) training split unless otherwise stated
notes: Spec mentions only `validation_samples: 512` for α-search (line 388), which is the Swift-SVD+ validation set, not the AA-SVD calibration. The latter is implicit (inherited from Stage 2).

spec_paraphrase: (spec doesn't specify a sequence length for AA-SVD covariance accumulation)
paper_verbatim: in our setting with 256 samples of length 2048
notes: Inherited from Stage 2 calibration (also unspecified at this section).

spec_paraphrase: (no ablation reasoning quoted)
paper_verbatim: perplexity drops sharply with the first ∼64 samples and largely saturates thereafter
notes: Background only.

spec_paraphrase: (no optimizer/lr/epochs in Stage 3 — block refinement absent)
paper_verbatim: AdamW ... lr 10^{-4}, 25 epochs ... cosine ... linear warmup, batch size 32
notes: Paper's exact block-refinement hyperparameters cannot be applied because the step itself is missing.

spec_paraphrase: (no offload between blocks — Stage 3 spills B/C covariance per layer instead, line 359)
paper_verbatim: forward passes can be executed in batches on GPU with the resulting activations offloaded to CPU memory between blocks
notes: Spec uses streaming covariance accumulation + per-layer disk spill — different but defensible memory strategy. Not relevant absent block refinement.

spec_paraphrase: B and C identical for gate_proj and up_proj within the same expert; eigendecomposition of B and rhs product cached once per expert via _precompute_eigh and reused. Q/K/V/O not in scope (attention protected, line 670).
paper_verbatim: multiple linear layers can share the same covariance matrix if they operate on the same input distribution (e.g. query, key and value projections or MLP gate and up projections within the same block)
notes: Spec applies the optimization to gate/up only (Q/K/V are protected attention).

spec_paraphrase: C is accumulated as X_pre^T @ X_post per batch; per-layer spill with background I/O thread.
paper_verbatim: computed in batches and by additively accumulating the outer products ... without explicitly materializing the full activation matrices

spec_paraphrase: Reduces parameters from d_out·d_in to k·(d_out + d_in); ω = n_experts·(d_out + d_in) is per-rank parameter cost.
paper_verbatim: ρ = (mk + nk) / (mn)

spec_paraphrase: (attention is protected — Stage 3 doesn't compress Q/K/V/O so KV cache savings don't apply)
paper_verbatim: Since attention projections are compressed, the activations stored in the cache scale with k
notes: Not applicable — spec only compresses MLP experts.

spec_paraphrase: (spec uses standard ratio convention; no Dobi-style half-precision remapping)
paper_verbatim: ρ = max(m, n)·k/(mn) = k/min(m, n)
notes: Spec sticks to standard ρ; AA-SVDq variant not used.

spec_paraphrase: (spec uses anchored-adaptive Path 1 without block-level refinement)
paper_verbatim: block-level joint optimization is the dominant factor driving final quality ... input-aware objective ... 6.89 PPL ... at ratio 0.8, and 8.35 PPL at ratio 0.6
notes: Paper's headline quality numbers (Table 1) are achievable ONLY with block refinement + input-aware objective. Spec's Path 1 (anchored-adaptive without refinement) corresponds to Table 5 row '0.8 ‖WX−W'X'‖²_F ✗' = PPL 7.68 and '0.6 ✗' = 12.19 — substantially worse than the paper-headline 6.89/8.35.

spec_paraphrase: (spec uses anchored-adaptive Path 1, NOT input-aware + refinement)
paper_verbatim: our method pairs the input-aware objective with block-level refinement
notes: Spec implements a strictly different recipe than the paper's final recommendation: (a) wrong layer objective (anchored-adaptive vs. paper's input-aware), (b) missing block-refinement. §12 D6 only sanctions the cross-covariance scope reduction, not these two deviations.

spec_paraphrase: (no ablation table)
(no ablation table)
notes: Input-aware + refine = best in paper. Spec ships neither.

spec_paraphrase: (no ablation table)
(no ablation table)
notes: This is the recipe the spec implements (✗-refinement column): PPL 7.68 — worse than the paper-headline 6.89.

spec_paraphrase: (no headline-number cite)
notes: Headline AA-SVD numbers presuppose block-refinement (see #paper_recommendation); spec cannot legitimately claim them.

spec_paraphrase: (no headline-number cite)
(no headline-number cite)
(no Dobi-remap)
(no model-zoo numbers)
paper_verbatim: 0.8 AA-SVD LLaMA-2-7B PPL 6.84 Acc 0.50 ; 0.6 PPL 8.55 Acc 0.44

spec_paraphrase: (no model-zoo numbers)
(no Qwen baseline cite)
notes: Stage 6 evals against Qwen3.6-35B-A3B (different model) so direct cite not required.

spec_paraphrase: (no memory-budget table)
Spec uses non-uniform per-projection-type rank: D-Rank Eq. 7 + per-projection multipliers (gate=1.33, up=0.67, down=1.0) + Swift-SVD+ per-expert redistribution.
paper_verbatim: Our current method applies a uniform compression ratio across all layers, with per-layer ranks derived from that ratio rather than optimized individually.
notes: AA-SVD itself has no per-projection bias; spec's non-uniform rank comes from D-Rank (2509.25622) + jangq's bit-allocation insight (D7a). Sanctioned by Ch.12 D7a (TODO empirical_pending).

spec_paraphrase: Spec uses paper-exact end-to-end PPL validation for α (Swift-SVD+ §3.2.2) — but applies it WITHOUT block-level refinement, so each candidate's evaluation does NOT include the refinement step that AA-SVD §5 specifically warns must be present for sensitivity-style search to be meaningful.
paper_verbatim: sensitivity-based approaches evaluate each layer in isolation, but our final compressed factors emerge from block-level joint refinement, making per-layer sensitivity a poor proxy
notes: AA-SVD §5 warns that per-layer sensitivity rank search is mismatched with block refinement. Spec's α-search picks ranks via PPL on a model that has no block refinement (because Stage 3 doesn't do it), so the warning is inverted but related: ranks chosen here may be sub-optimal once any future refinement is added, and conversely the absence of refinement makes the picked ranks fragile. No Ch.12 row addresses this.

spec_paraphrase: Both teacher (original) and student (post Stage-2.5 partially compressed) loaded simultaneously in VRAM (~120 GB on H200). Hooks on both collect X_pre and X_post.
paper_verbatim: X' the corresponding shifted activations—collected by running the same calibration samples through the partially compressed network up to (but not including) the current component
notes: Spec uses Stage 2.5 student as 'partially compressed' frozen state, rather than block-by-block iterative compression. Functionally equivalent for the cross-covariance capture, modulo within-block sequential compression (#alg2_inner).

spec_paraphrase: 'What would the original model have produced for the inputs the compressed model actually receives' — same anchoring semantics.
paper_verbatim: anchoring the compressed network to the behavior of the uncompressed one while simultaneously adapting to the shifted input distribution

spec_paraphrase: (no critique of shift-aware variant)
paper_verbatim: anchoring solely to X' risks amplifying divergence ... noisy on finite calibration
notes: Background only.

spec_paraphrase: (no critique cited)
paper_verbatim: input-aware methods do not account for this error accumulation

spec_paraphrase: U·V representation gives reduced-FLOP forward pass via standard linear algebra.
paper_verbatim: (UV^T)X = U(V^T X)

spec_paraphrase: (spec targets Qwen3.6-35B-A3B MoE — different from paper's dense Qwen-2.5-7B)
paper_verbatim: LLaMA-2-7B, LLaMA-2-13B, LLaMA-3-1B, LLaMA-3-8B, Qwen-2.5-7B
notes: Paper validates on dense LLaMA/Qwen; spec applies to MoE. AA-SVD has no published MoE validation — known extrapolation.

spec_paraphrase: Spec evaluates ARC-C and HellaSwag (paper subset) plus HumanEval and MATH-500 (not in paper).
paper_verbatim: WinoGrande, PIQA, ARC-Easy, ARC-Challenge, OpenBookQA, HellaSwag, MathQA
notes: Spec uses 2 of 7 paper benchmarks + 2 code/math benchmarks — different evaluation suite, but acceptable since Stage 6 sets its own quality gates.

spec_paraphrase: Spec evaluates WikiText-2 PPL only (no C4, no PTB).
paper_verbatim: WikiText2, C4, PTB
notes: WikiText-2 is the canonical PPL benchmark in this lineage; subset acceptable.

spec_paraphrase: (no per-layer error tracking)
paper_verbatim: AA-SVD achieves the lowest error throughout the network

spec_paraphrase: (no ablation table)
(no ablation table)
notes: Input-aware+refine = best at ρ=0.6 (8.35 PPL).

spec_paraphrase: (no ablation table)
notes: Spec's recipe (anchored-adaptive without refinement, ρ≈0.6) → expected PPL ~12.19, vs. paper-headline 8.35.

spec_paraphrase: Spec cites AA-SVD by Theorem 3.2 + Corollary 3.3 only; the paper's second-and-more-critical contribution (block-level refinement) is uncited and unimplemented.
paper_verbatim: Second, and more critically, we introduced block-level refinement
notes: Authors' own ranking: block-refinement > layer objective. Spec inverts this priority by citing only the layer-objective contribution.
